name: Wifi-Reconaissance-Program
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``Wifi-Reconaissance-Program`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: python3 snifferx1.py   (or python3 snifferx2.py; snifferx2.py crashes with KeyError due to ip/MAC vs IP/MAC key mismatch)

examples.command: python3 snifferx1.py   (or python3 snifferx2.py; snifferx2.py crashes with KeyError due to ip/MAC vs IP/MAC key mismatch)

algorithms: ARP broadcast sweep for live hosts on local /24 (scapy ARP + Ether srp)
IP-layer packet sniffer via scapy.sniff printing src/dst/proto
Raw payload decode (utf-8) of packets with Raw layer
Public IP lookup via api64.ipify.org HTTP API
produces: stdout list of connected devices (IP, MAC) from ARP sweep
stdout packet log: src_ip -> dst_ip | protocol number, plus decoded Raw payload when present
stdout local IP, default gateway IP, public IP
novelty: none — thin wrapper. It is literally scapy.ARP srp + scapy.sniff with print statements. No 802.11 monitor mode, no management-frame parsing, no beacon/probe/EAPOL handling, no airodump-ng/wash/hcxtools integration, no WiFi-specific recon at all — it operates on the encrypted/data layer of an associated station, not on WiFi. snifferx2.py additionally has a KeyError bug (keys stored as ip/mac, read as IP/MAC) and a broken placeholder range "192.168.12x.1/2x".

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
